843	
844	    # Set projected separator color from config
845	    DAILY_PROJECTED_COLOR=$(get_color_code "$DAILY_PROJECTED_BAR_COLOR")
846	
847	    # Set current progress bar color from config
848	    DAILY_COLOR=$(get_color_code "$DAILY_BAR_COLOR")
849	
850	    # Build progress bar with colored projection separator
851	    DAILY_PROGRESS_BAR="["
852	    for ((i=0; i<BAR_LENGTH; i++)); do
853	        if [ $i -lt $DAILY_FILLED ]; then
854	            DAILY_PROGRESS_BAR="${DAILY_PROGRESS_BAR}█"
855	        elif [ $i -eq $DAILY_PROJECTED_POS ]; then
856	            # Projection separator uses current layer color (displayed on current bar)
857	            DAILY_PROGRESS_BAR="${DAILY_PROGRESS_BAR}${RESET_CODE}${DAILY_COLOR}│${RESET_CODE}${DAILY_COLOR}"
858	        else
859	            DAILY_PROGRESS_BAR="${DAILY_PROGRESS_BAR}░"
860	        fi
861	    done
862	
863	    # Handle separator at end position (when DAILY_PROJECTED_POS == BAR_LENGTH)
864	    # This occurs when projection crosses layer boundary and is capped
865	    if [ $DAILY_PROJECTED_POS -eq $BAR_LENGTH ]; then
866	        DAILY_PROGRESS_BAR="${DAILY_PROGRESS_BAR}${RESET_CODE}${DAILY_COLOR}│${RESET_CODE}${DAILY_COLOR}"
867	    fi
868	
869	    DAILY_PROGRESS_BAR="${DAILY_PROGRESS_BAR}]"
870	
871	    # Format daily percentage for display (rounded to whole number)
872	    DAILY_PCT_DISPLAY=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.0f\", $DAILY_PCT}")
873	fi
874	
875	# ====================================================================================
876	# WEEKLY_TRACKER (weekly subsection - shows weekly usage/avail/recommend)
877	# ====================================================================================
878	if [ "$SHOW_WEEKLY" = "true" ] && [ -n "${WEEKLY_PCT:-}" ]; then
879	    # Calculate display value and label based on mode
880	    case "$WEEKLY_DISPLAY_MODE" in
881	        "avail")
882	            WEEKLY_DISPLAY_VALUE=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.0f\", 100 - $WEEKLY_PCT}")
883	            WEEKLY_LABEL="avail"
884	            ;;
885	        "recommend")
886	            # Calculate recommended daily usage for stable budgeting
887	            # Formula: (weekly_limit - usage_from_weekly_start_to_daily_cycle_start) / cycles_left
888	            # This gives budget available at current daily cycle start (e.g., 3pm today)
889	            # Stays stable throughout the day, updates only at daily cycle reset
890	
891	            if [ -z "${RESET_TIMESTAMP:-}" ] && [ -n "$OFFICIAL_RESET_DATE" ] && type iso_to_timestamp &>/dev/null; then
892	                RESET_TIMESTAMP=$(iso_to_timestamp "$OFFICIAL_RESET_DATE")
893	            fi
894	
895	            if [ -n "${RESET_TIMESTAMP:-}" ] && type get_anthropic_period &>/dev/null && type get_daily_period &>/dev/null; then
896	                # Get weekly and daily period boundaries
897	                PERIOD_DATA=$(get_anthropic_period "$RESET_TIMESTAMP")
898	                WEEKLY_START=$(echo "$PERIOD_DATA" | jq -r '.start')
899	                NEXT_RESET=$(echo "$PERIOD_DATA" | jq -r '.end')
900	
901	                DAILY_PERIOD=$(get_daily_period "$RESET_TIMESTAMP")
902	                DAILY_CYCLE_START=$(echo "$DAILY_PERIOD" | jq -r '.start')
903	
904	                # Calculate usage from weekly start to current daily cycle start
905	                # This captures all completed daily cycles in the week
906	                WEEKLY_START_ISO=$(timestamp_to_iso "$WEEKLY_START")
907	                DAILY_CYCLE_START_ISO=$(timestamp_to_iso "$DAILY_CYCLE_START")
908	
909	                # Query ccusage blocks for costs between weekly start and daily cycle start
910	                BLOCKS_DATA=$(cd ~ && npx --yes ccusage blocks --json --offline 2>/dev/null | awk '/^{/,0')
911	
912	                if [ -n "$BLOCKS_DATA" ] && [ "$BLOCKS_DATA" != "null" ]; then
913	                    COST_BEFORE_CYCLE=$(echo "$BLOCKS_DATA" | jq -r --arg start "$WEEKLY_START_ISO" --arg end "$DAILY_CYCLE_START_ISO" '
914	                        [.blocks[] |
915	                         select(
916	                           (.startTime >= $start) and
917	                           (.startTime < $end)
918	                         ) |
919	                         .costUSD
920	                        ] | add // 0
921	                    ')
922	
923	                    # Weekly available at current daily cycle start
924	                    WEEKLY_AVAIL_AT_CYCLE_START=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", $WEEKLY_LIMIT - $COST_BEFORE_CYCLE}")
925	                    WEEKLY_AVAIL_PCT=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", ($WEEKLY_AVAIL_AT_CYCLE_START / $WEEKLY_LIMIT) * 100}")
926	
927	                    # Calculate cycles left from now to NEXT reset
928	                    CURRENT_TIME=$(date +%s)
929	                    TIME_UNTIL_RESET=$((NEXT_RESET - CURRENT_TIME))
930	                    CYCLES_LEFT=$(awk "BEGIN {hours = $TIME_UNTIL_RESET / 3600; cycles = hours / 24; print (cycles == int(cycles)) ? int(cycles) : int(cycles) + 1}")
931	
932	                    if [ "$CYCLES_LEFT" -gt 0 ]; then
933	                        WEEKLY_DISPLAY_VALUE=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.0f\", $WEEKLY_AVAIL_PCT / $CYCLES_LEFT}")
934	                    else
935	                        WEEKLY_DISPLAY_VALUE="0"
936	                    fi
937	                else
938	                    # Fallback to usage if data unavailable
939	                    WEEKLY_DISPLAY_VALUE="$WEEKLY_PCT"
940	                fi
941	            else
942	                # Fallback to usage if data unavailable
943	                WEEKLY_DISPLAY_VALUE="$WEEKLY_PCT"
944	            fi
945	            WEEKLY_LABEL="recom"
946	            ;;
947	        *)
948	            # Default: usage mode
949	            WEEKLY_DISPLAY_VALUE="$WEEKLY_PCT"
950	            WEEKLY_LABEL="weekly"
951	            ;;
952	    esac
953	
954	    # Match color with daily section if daily is enabled, otherwise use layer logic
955	    if [ "$SHOW_DAILY" = "true" ] && [ -n "${DAILY_BAR_COLOR:-}" ]; then
956	        # Use same color as daily section
957	        WEEKLY_COLOR=$(get_color_code "$DAILY_BAR_COLOR")
958	    else
959	        # Apply layer logic directly to weekly percentage using static base
960	        WEEKLY_STATIC_BASE=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", ($WEEKLY_LIMIT / 7.0) / $WEEKLY_LIMIT * 100}")
961	        WEEKLY_LAYER1=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", $WEEKLY_STATIC_BASE * $DAILY_LAYER1_THRESHOLD_MULT}")
962	
963	        if (( $(awk "BEGIN {print ($WEEKLY_PCT <= $WEEKLY_LAYER1)}") )); then
964	            WEEKLY_COLOR=$(get_color_code "$DAILY_LAYER1_COLOR")
965	        else
966	            WEEKLY_COLOR=$(get_color_code "$DAILY_LAYER2_COLOR")
967	        fi
968	    fi
969	fi
970	
971	# ====================================================================================
972	# MONTHLY COST SECTION (independent)
973	# ====================================================================================
974	if [ "$SHOW_MONTHLY" = "true" ] && [ -n "$PAYMENT_CYCLE_START_DATE" ] && type get_monthly_cost &>/dev/null; then
975	    # Convert payment cycle start date to Unix timestamp
976	    PAYMENT_CYCLE_TIMESTAMP=$(iso_to_timestamp "$PAYMENT_CYCLE_START_DATE")
977	
978	    # Get monthly cost with caching
979	    MONTHLY_COST=$(get_monthly_cost "$PAYMENT_CYCLE_TIMESTAMP" "$CACHE_DURATION")
980	
981	    # Format monthly cost display
982	    if [ -n "$MONTHLY_COST" ] && [ "$MONTHLY_COST" != "0" ]; then
983	        MONTHLY_COST_DISPLAY=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.0f\", $MONTHLY_COST}")
984	    fi
985	fi
986	
987	# ====================================================================================
988	# SESSIONS SECTION (independent)
989	# ====================================================================================
990	if [ "$SHOW_SESSIONS" = "true" ]; then
991	    # Count concurrent Claude Code sessions (projects with activity in last N minutes)
992	    # Expand tilde in path
993	    PROJECTS_PATH="${CLAUDE_PROJECTS_PATH/#\~/$HOME}"
994	    ACTIVE_SESSIONS=$(find "$PROJECTS_PATH" -name "*.jsonl" -type f -mmin -$SESSION_ACTIVITY_THRESHOLD 2>/dev/null | \
995	        xargs -I {} dirname {} 2>/dev/null | sort -u | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
996	fi
997	
998	# ====================================================================================
999	# STAGE 3: RENDERING - FINAL ASSEMBLY
1000	# ====================================================================================
1001	# Assemble all computed sections into the final statusline output.
1002	# Each section has already completed its own data→compute→render pipeline.
1003	# This stage conditionally includes sections based on:
1004	#   - Section toggle settings (show_*)
1005	#   - Data availability (presence of computed values)
1006	# ====================================================================================
1007	
1008	STATUSLINE_SECTIONS=()
1009	
1010	# Directory section
1011	[[ "$SHOW_DIRECTORY" == "true" ]] && STATUSLINE_SECTIONS+=("${ORANGE_CODE}${DIR_NAME}${RESET_CODE}")
1012	
1013	# Model section
1014	[[ -n "${MODEL_SHORT:-}" ]] && STATUSLINE_SECTIONS+=("${DIM_BLUE_CODE}${MODEL_SHORT}${RESET_CODE}")
1015	
1016	[[ "$SHOW_CONTEXT" == "true" ]] && [[ -n "${CTX_TOTAL:-}" ]] && STATUSLINE_SECTIONS+=("${CTX_COLOR}${CTX_TOTAL} ${CTX_PROGRESS_BAR}${RESET_CODE}")
1017	[[ "$SHOW_FIVE_HOUR_WINDOW" == "true" ]] && [[ -n "${COST_FMT:-}" ]] && STATUSLINE_SECTIONS+=("${PROGRESS_COLOR}${COST_FMT} ${PROGRESS_BAR} ${COST_PERCENTAGE}%${RESET_CODE}")
1018	
1019	# Add daily/weekly combined or separate sections
1020	if [[ "$SHOW_DAILY" == "true" ]] && [[ "$SHOW_WEEKLY" == "true" ]] && [[ "$WEEKLY_DISPLAY_MODE" == "recommend" ]] && [[ -n "${DAILY_PROGRESS_BAR:-}" ]] && [[ -n "${WEEKLY_DISPLAY_VALUE:-}" ]]; then
1021	    # Combined mode: daily [bar] actual/recommend% $actual/$recommend (with dimmed portions)
1022	    DIM_CODE="\033[2m"
1023	
1024	    # Calculate recommend daily cost from precise dollar amount (not rounded percentage)
1025	    # This avoids rounding error: $850/7 = $121 (not 14% × $850 = $119)
1026	    if [ -n "${WEEKLY_AVAIL_AT_CYCLE_START:-}" ] && [ -n "${CYCLES_LEFT:-}" ] && [ "$CYCLES_LEFT" -gt 0 ]; then
1027	        RECOMMEND_DAILY_COST=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.0f\", $WEEKLY_AVAIL_AT_CYCLE_START / $CYCLES_LEFT}")
1028	    else
1029	        # Fallback to percentage-based calculation
1030	        RECOMMEND_DAILY_COST=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.0f\", ($WEEKLY_DISPLAY_VALUE / 100) * $WEEKLY_LIMIT}")
1031	    fi
1032	
1033	    DAILY_COST_DISPLAY=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.0f\", $DAILY_COST}")
1034	
1035	    # Conditionally show "daily " label
1036	    if [[ "$SHOW_LABELS" == "true" ]]; then
1037	        STATUSLINE_SECTIONS+=("${DAILY_COLOR}daily ${DAILY_PROGRESS_BAR} ${DAILY_PCT_DISPLAY}${DIM_CODE}/${WEEKLY_DISPLAY_VALUE}% \$${DAILY_COST_DISPLAY}/\$${RECOMMEND_DAILY_COST}${RESET_CODE}")
1038	    else
1039	        STATUSLINE_SECTIONS+=("${DAILY_COLOR}${DAILY_PROGRESS_BAR} ${DAILY_PCT_DISPLAY}${DIM_CODE}/${WEEKLY_DISPLAY_VALUE}% \$${DAILY_COST_DISPLAY}/\$${RECOMMEND_DAILY_COST}${RESET_CODE}")
1040	    fi
1041	elif [[ "$SHOW_DAILY" == "true" ]] && [[ -n "${DAILY_PROGRESS_BAR:-}" ]]; then
1042	    # Daily only
1043	    if [[ "$SHOW_LABELS" == "true" ]]; then
1044	        STATUSLINE_SECTIONS+=("${DAILY_COLOR}daily ${DAILY_PROGRESS_BAR} ${DAILY_PCT_DISPLAY}%${RESET_CODE}")
1045	    else
1046	        STATUSLINE_SECTIONS+=("${DAILY_COLOR}${DAILY_PROGRESS_BAR} ${DAILY_PCT_DISPLAY}%${RESET_CODE}")
1047	    fi
1048	fi
1049	
1050	# Weekly section (only if not already combined with daily)
1051	if [[ "$SHOW_WEEKLY" == "true" ]] && [[ -n "${WEEKLY_DISPLAY_VALUE:-}" ]]; then
1052	    if [[ "$SHOW_DAILY" != "true" ]] || [[ "$WEEKLY_DISPLAY_MODE" != "recommend" ]]; then
1053	        # Conditionally show weekly label
1054	        if [[ "$SHOW_LABELS" == "true" ]]; then
1055	            STATUSLINE_SECTIONS+=("${WEEKLY_COLOR}${WEEKLY_LABEL} ${WEEKLY_DISPLAY_VALUE}%${RESET_CODE}")
1056	        else
1057	            STATUSLINE_SECTIONS+=("${WEEKLY_COLOR}${WEEKLY_DISPLAY_VALUE}%${RESET_CODE}")
1058	        fi
1059	    fi
1060	fi
1061	
1062	# Monthly cost section (between weekly and timer)
1063	if [[ "$SHOW_MONTHLY" == "true" ]] && [[ -n "${MONTHLY_COST_DISPLAY:-}" ]]; then
1064	    # Conditionally show "total " label
1065	    if [[ "$SHOW_LABELS" == "true" ]]; then
1066	        STATUSLINE_SECTIONS+=("${DIM_BLUE_CODE}total \$${MONTHLY_COST_DISPLAY}${RESET_CODE}")
1067	    else
1068	        STATUSLINE_SECTIONS+=("${DIM_BLUE_CODE}\$${MONTHLY_COST_DISPLAY}${RESET_CODE}")
1069	    fi
1070	fi
1071	
1072	[[ "$SHOW_TIMER" == "true" ]] && [[ -n "${RESET_INFO:-}" ]] && STATUSLINE_SECTIONS+=("${PURPLE_CODE}${RESET_INFO}${RESET_CODE}")
1073	[[ "$SHOW_TOKEN_RATE" == "true" ]] && [[ -n "${TOKEN_RATE:-}" ]] && STATUSLINE_SECTIONS+=("${CYAN_CODE}${TOKEN_RATE}${RESET_CODE}")
1074	[[ "$SHOW_SESSIONS" == "true" ]] && [[ -n "${ACTIVE_SESSIONS:-}" ]] && STATUSLINE_SECTIONS+=("${CYAN_CODE}×${ACTIVE_SESSIONS}${RESET_CODE}")
1075	
1076	# Join sections with separator
1077	# Define dimmed separator
1078	DIM_CODE="\033[2m"
1079	SEPARATOR=" ${DIM_CODE}|${RESET_CODE} "
1080	
1081	STATUSLINE=""
1082	FIRST=true
1083	for section in "${STATUSLINE_SECTIONS[@]}"; do
1084	    if [[ "$FIRST" == "true" ]]; then
1085	        STATUSLINE="$section"
1086	        FIRST=false
1087	    else
1088	        STATUSLINE="$STATUSLINE$SEPARATOR$section"
1089	    fi
1090	done
1091	
1092	# Display statusline
1093	if [ -n "$STATUSLINE" ]; then
1094	    printf '%b\n' "$STATUSLINE"
1095	else
1096	    # Fallback if no sections enabled
1097	    echo "$DIR_NAME | No active window"
1098	fi
1099

content.text: Now let me also check the utility functions to understand how `get_monthly_cost` works.

content.content:
1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# statusline-utils.sh - Utility functions for official Anthropic weekly reset tracking
3	# This file provides ccusage_r scheme support for matching official console reset schedule
4	
5	# Constants
6	readonly WEEK_DURATION_SECONDS=604800  # 7 days
7	readonly DAY_DURATION_SECONDS=86400    # 24 hours
8	
9	# Convert Unix timestamp to ISO 8601 format
10	# Usage: timestamp_to_iso <timestamp>
11	# Returns: ISO 8601 string like "2025-10-01T15:00:00Z"
12	timestamp_to_iso() {
13	    local timestamp="${1:?Missing timestamp}"
14	    date -u -r "$timestamp" +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ" 2>/dev/null || \
15	        date -u -d "@$timestamp" +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ" 2>/dev/null || true
16	}
17	
18	# Convert ISO 8601 timestamp to Unix timestamp
19	# Usage: iso_to_timestamp <iso_string>
20	# Returns: Unix timestamp (seconds since epoch)
21	iso_to_timestamp() {
22	    local iso_string="${1:?Missing ISO timestamp}"
23	    # Normalise timezone colon (+00:00 → +0000) for macOS %z compatibility
24	    local iso_normalised=$(echo "$iso_string" | sed 's/\([+-][0-9][0-9]\):\([0-9][0-9]\)$/\1\2/')
25	    # macOS (BSD date) - using -j prevents setting system time, -f specifies input format
26	    date -j -f "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z" "$iso_normalised" "+%s" 2>/dev/null || \
27	        date -j -u -f "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S" "${iso_string%%[+-Z]*}" "+%s" 2>/dev/null || \
28	        # GNU date - simpler syntax
29	        date -d "$iso_string" "+%s" 2>/dev/null || true
30	}
31	
32	# Calculate current Anthropic weekly period boundaries
33	# Anthropic uses fixed reset cycles (e.g., Wed 3pm → Wed 3pm in America/Vancouver timezone)
34	# Usage: get_anthropic_period <next_reset_timestamp>
35	# Returns: JSON with period start, end, and progress percentage
36	get_anthropic_period() {
37	    local next_reset="${1:?Missing next_reset timestamp}"
38	    local current_ts=$(date +%s)
39	
40	    # If we're past the reset time, calculate the next period
41	    if [[ $current_ts -ge $next_reset ]]; then
42	        # Calculate how many weeks past the reset we are
43	        local weeks_past=$(( (current_ts - next_reset) / WEEK_DURATION_SECONDS ))
44	        next_reset=$(( next_reset + (weeks_past + 1) * WEEK_DURATION_SECONDS ))
45	    # If we're before the reset, we might need to go back to find the current period
46	    elif [[ $current_ts -lt $(( next_reset - WEEK_DURATION_SECONDS )) ]]; then
47	        # Go back to find the right period
48	        while [[ $current_ts -lt $(( next_reset - WEEK_DURATION_SECONDS )) ]]; do
49	            next_reset=$(( next_reset - WEEK_DURATION_SECONDS ))
50	        done
51	    fi
52	
53	    # Current period: [reset - 7 days, reset)
54	    local period_start=$(( next_reset - WEEK_DURATION_SECONDS ))
55	    local period_end=$next_reset
56	
57	    # Calculate progress
58	    local elapsed=$(( current_ts - period_start ))
59	    local percent=$(( elapsed * 100 / WEEK_DURATION_SECONDS ))
60	
61	    printf '{"start":%d,"end":%d,"percent":%d}\n' \
62	        "$period_start" "$period_end" "$percent"
63	}

65	# Calculate current daily period based on reset time
66	# Daily periods reset at the same time each day (e.g., 3pm daily for a 3pm weekly reset)
67	# Usage: get_daily_period <next_reset_timestamp>
68	# Returns: JSON with period start and current time
69	get_daily_period() {
70	    local next_reset="${1:?Missing next_reset timestamp}"
71	    local current_ts=$(date +%s)
72	
73	    # Extract the time-of-day from the reset timestamp
74	    # Strategy: Get the hour offset from midnight in the reset's timezone
75	
76	    # First, get the weekly period to understand the reset schedule
77	    local weekly_period=$(get_anthropic_period "$next_reset")
78	    local weekly_start=$(echo "$weekly_period" | jq -r '.start')
79	
80	    # Calculate seconds since start of week (day-of-week offset)
81	    local week_offset=$(( (current_ts - weekly_start) % WEEK_DURATION_SECONDS ))
82	
83	    # Calculate today's reset time by finding the last occurrence of the daily reset hour
84	    # Start from the weekly reset time and add days until we're close to now
85	    local daily_reset=$weekly_start
86	
87	    # Fast forward to approximately today (within this week)
88	    while [[ $((daily_reset + DAY_DURATION_SECONDS)) -le $current_ts ]]; do
89	        daily_reset=$((daily_reset + DAY_DURATION_SECONDS))
90	    done
91	
92	    # If we're before today's reset time, use yesterday's reset
93	    if [[ $current_ts -lt $daily_reset ]]; then
94	        daily_reset=$((daily_reset - DAY_DURATION_SECONDS))
95	    fi
96	
97	    printf '{"start":%d,"end":%d}\n' "$daily_reset" "$current_ts"
98	}
99	
100	# Calculate daily cost using ccusage blocks
101	# Filters blocks from today's baseline reset time to current time
102	# Usage: get_daily_cost <next_reset_timestamp> [cache_duration_seconds]
103	# Returns: Total cost for current daily period
104	get_daily_cost() {
105	    local next_reset="${1:?Missing next_reset timestamp}"
106	    local cache_duration="${2:-300}"  # Default 5 minutes, configurable
107	
108	    # Get script directory (when sourced, use caller's directory)
109	    local utils_dir="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
110	    local cache_file="$utils_dir/../data/.daily_cache"
111	    local cache_duration_minutes=$((cache_duration / 60))
112	
113	    # Get current daily period boundaries
114	    local period_data=$(get_daily_period "$next_reset")
115	    local period_start=$(echo "$period_data" | jq -r '.start')
116	    local period_end=$(echo "$period_data" | jq -r '.end')
117	
118	    # Check if cache exists and is fresh
119	    if [[ -f "$cache_file" ]]; then
120	        local cache_age=$(find "$cache_file" -mmin -${cache_duration_minutes} 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
121	        if [[ $cache_age -gt 0 ]]; then
122	            # Read cache and validate format: timestamp|period_start|period_end|daily_cost
123	            local cache_content=$(cat "$cache_file")
124	            local cached_period_start=$(echo "$cache_content" | cut -d'|' -f2)
125	            local cached_cost=$(echo "$cache_content" | cut -d'|' -f4)
126	
127	            # Validate: same period AND valid number
128	            if [[ "$cached_period_start" == "$period_start" ]] && [[ "$cached_cost" =~ ^[0-9]+\.?[0-9]*$ ]]; then
129	                echo "$cached_cost"
130	                return 0
131	            fi
132	            # Cache invalid (wrong period or corrupted) - fall through to recalculate
133	        fi
134	    fi
135	
136	    # Convert period start to ISO 8601 format for comparison with ccusage
137	    local start_iso=$(timestamp_to_iso "$period_start")
138	
139	    # Get all blocks from ccusage
140	    local blocks_data=$(cd ~ && npx --yes ccusage blocks --json --offline 2>/dev/null | awk '/^{/,0')
141	
142	    if [[ -z "$blocks_data" || "$blocks_data" == "null" ]]; then
143	        echo "0.00"
144	        return
145	    fi
146	
147	    # Filter blocks where startTime >= period_start OR blocks that overlap the period
148	    # For overlapping blocks, include the full cost (conservative estimate)
149	    local period_end_iso=$(timestamp_to_iso "$period_end")
150	    local daily_cost=$(echo "$blocks_data" | jq -r --arg start_iso "$start_iso" --arg end_iso "$period_end_iso" '
151	        [.blocks[] |
152	         select(
153	           # Block starts within daily period
154	           (.startTime >= $start_iso) or
155	           # OR block is active and overlaps with daily period (endTime > period_start)
156	           (.endTime > $start_iso and .startTime < $end_iso)
157	         ) |
158	         .costUSD
159	        ] | add // 0
160	    ')
161	
162	    # Format to 2 decimal places
163	    daily_cost=$(printf "%.2f" "$daily_cost")
164	
165	    # Atomic cache write with period metadata
166	    # Format: timestamp|period_start|period_end|daily_cost
167	    local current_ts=$(date +%s)
168	    echo "${current_ts}|${period_start}|${period_end}|${daily_cost}" > "${cache_file}.tmp"
169	    mv "${cache_file}.tmp" "$cache_file"
170	
171	    echo "$daily_cost"
172	}

223	    local weekly_cost=$(echo "$blocks_data" | jq -r --arg start_iso "$start_iso" '

234	    # Format: timestamp|period_start|period_end|weekly_cost
235	    local current_ts=$(date +%s)
236	    echo "${current_ts}|${period_start}|${period_end}|${weekly_cost}" > "${cache_file}.tmp"
237	    mv "${cache_file}.tmp" "$cache_file"

242	# Calculate weekly recommended usage based on cycle start
243	# Returns static value for current cycle: weekly_avail_at_cycle_start / ceil(cycles_left_from_cycle_start)
244	# Usage: get_weekly_recommend <next_reset_timestamp> <weekly_limit> <weekly_baseline_pct> [cache_duration_seconds]
245	# Returns: Recommended daily usage percentage
246	get_weekly_recommend() {
247	    local next_reset="${1:?Missing next_reset timestamp}"
248	    local weekly_limit="${2:?Missing weekly_limit}"
249	    local weekly_baseline_pct="${3:-0}"
250	    local cache_duration="${4:-300}"
251	
252	    # Cache file per cycle
253	    local cache_file="${CACHE_DIR:-.}/.weekly_recommend_cache"
254	
255	    # Get current cycle period
256	    local period_data=$(get_daily_period "$next_reset")
257	    local cycle_start=$(echo "$period_data" | jq -r '.start')
258	
259	    # Check cache validity
260	    if [[ -f "$cache_file" ]]; then
261	        IFS='|' read -r cached_ts cached_cycle_start cached_value < "$cache_file"
262	        local current_ts=$(date +%s)
263	
264	        # Cache valid if: same cycle AND within cache duration
265	        if [[ "$cached_cycle_start" == "$cycle_start" ]] && \
266	           [[ $((current_ts - cached_ts)) -lt $cache_duration ]] && \
267	           [[ "$cached_value" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
268	            echo "$cached_value"
269	            return 0
270	        fi
271	    fi
272	
273	    # Get weekly cost at cycle start by filtering blocks
274	    local cycle_start_iso=$(timestamp_to_iso "$cycle_start")
275	    local weekly_period=$(get_anthropic_period "$next_reset")
276	    local period_start=$(echo "$weekly_period" | jq -r '.start')
277	    local period_start_iso=$(timestamp_to_iso "$period_start")
278	
279	    # Get blocks and filter up to cycle start
280	    local blocks_data=$(cd ~ && npx --yes ccusage blocks --json --offline 2>/dev/null | awk '/^{/,0')
281	
282	    if [[ -z "$blocks_data" || "$blocks_data" == "null" ]]; then
283	        echo "0"
284	        return
285	    fi
286	
287	    # Calculate weekly cost at cycle start (from blocks before cycle started)
288	    local weekly_cost_at_cycle_start=$(echo "$blocks_data" | jq -r --arg start_iso "$period_start_iso" --arg end_iso "$cycle_start_iso" '
289	        [.blocks[] |
290	         select(
291	           (.startTime >= $start_iso) and
292	           (.startTime < $end_iso)
293	         ) |
294	         .costUSD
295	        ] | add // 0
296	    ')
297	
298	    # Apply baseline offset (same as current week calculation)
299	    if [ "$(awk "BEGIN {print ($weekly_baseline_pct != 0)}")" = "1" ]; then
300	        local baseline_cost=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", ($weekly_limit * $weekly_baseline_pct) / 100}")

302	    fi

304	    # Calculate available budget at cycle start
305	    local avail_cost_at_start=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", $weekly_limit - $weekly_cost_at_cycle_start}")
306	    local avail_pct_at_start=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", ($avail_cost_at_start / $weekly_limit) * 100}")

309	    local time_from_cycle_start_to_reset=$((next_reset - cycle_start))

312	    # Calculate recommend value

326	# Calculate monthly period boundaries
327	# Returns current monthly period from payment_cycle_start_date to next month
328	# Usage: get_monthly_period <payment_cycle_start_timestamp>
329	# Returns: JSON with {start, end} timestamps
330	get_monthly_period() {
331	    local cycle_start="${1:?Missing payment_cycle_start timestamp}"
332	
333	    # Get current time
334	    local current_time=$(date +%s)
335	
336	    # Extract date components from cycle start
337	    local cycle_start_iso=$(timestamp_to_iso "$cycle_start")
338	
339	    # Try GNU date first, then macOS date
340	    # Normalise ISO string for macOS date -j: Z → +0000, strip colon in +HH:MM
341	    local cycle_start_iso_norm=$(echo "$cycle_start_iso" | sed 's/Z$/+0000/; s/\([+-][0-9][0-9]\):\([0-9][0-9]\)$/\1\2/')
342	
343	    local cycle_day=$(date -d "$cycle_start_iso" "+%d" 2>/dev/null) || true
344	    if [ -z "$cycle_day" ]; then
345	        cycle_day=$(date -j -f "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z" "$cycle_start_iso_norm" "+%d" 2>/dev/null) || true
346	    fi
347	    if [ -z "$cycle_day" ]; then
348	        cycle_day=$(date -j -u -f "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S" "${cycle_start_iso%%[Z+]*}" "+%d" 2>/dev/null)
349	    fi
350	
351	    local cycle_time=$(date -d "$cycle_start_iso" "+%H:%M:%S" 2>/dev/null) || true
352	    if [ -z "$cycle_time" ]; then
353	        cycle_time=$(date -j -f "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z" "$cycle_start_iso_norm" "+%H:%M:%S" 2>/dev/null) || true
354	    fi
355	    if [ -z "$cycle_time" ]; then

367	        current_month=$(date -j -r "$current_time" "+%m")
368	    fi
369	
370	    # Build current month's cycle start (same day and time, current month)
371	    local this_month_cycle=$(date -d "${current_year}-${current_month}-${cycle_day} ${cycle_time}" "+%s" 2>/dev/null) || true
372	    if [ -z "$this_month_cycle" ]; then

374	    fi
375	
376	    # If current time is before this month's cycle start, use previous month
377	    if [ "$current_time" -lt "$this_month_cycle" ]; then

388	        fi
389	    fi
390	
391	    # Calculate next month's cycle start
392	    local next_year="$current_year"
393	    local next_month=$(printf "%02d" $((10#$current_month + 1)))
394	    if [ "$next_month" = "13" ]; then
395	        next_year=$((current_year + 1))
396	        next_month="01"
397	    fi
398	
399	    local next_month_cycle=$(date -d "${next_year}-${next_month}-${cycle_day} ${cycle_time}" "+%s" 2>/dev/null) || true
400	    if [ -z "$next_month_cycle" ]; then
401	        next_month_cycle=$(date -j -f "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S" "${next_year}-${next_month}-${cycle_day} ${cycle_time}" "+%s" 2>/dev/null)
402	    fi
403	
404	    # Return period boundaries
405	    echo "{\"start\": $this_month_cycle, \"end\": $next_month_cycle}"
406	}
407	
408	# Calculate monthly cost using ccusage blocks
409	# Filters blocks from payment cycle start to current time
410	# Usage: get_monthly_cost <payment_cycle_start_timestamp> [cache_duration_seconds]
411	# Returns: Total cost for current monthly period
412	get_monthly_cost() {
413	    local cycle_start="${1:?Missing payment_cycle_start timestamp}"
414	    local cache_duration="${2:-300}"  # Default 5 minutes
415	
416	    # Get script directory
417	    local utils_dir="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
418	    local cache_file="$utils_dir/../data/.monthly_cache"
419	    local cache_duration_minutes=$((cache_duration / 60))
420	
421	    # Get current monthly period boundaries
422	    local period_data=$(get_monthly_period "$cycle_start")
423	    local period_start=$(echo "$period_data" | jq -r '.start')
424	    local period_end=$(echo "$period_data" | jq -r '.end')
425	
426	    # Check if cache exists and is fresh
427	    if [[ -f "$cache_file" ]]; then
428	        local cache_age=$(find "$cache_file" -mmin -${cache_duration_minutes} 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
429	        if [[ $cache_age -gt 0 ]]; then
430	            # Read cache and validate format: timestamp|period_start|period_end|monthly_cost
431	            local cache_content=$(cat "$cache_file")
432	            local cached_period_start=$(echo "$cache_content" | cut -d'|' -f2)
433	            local cached_cost=$(echo "$cache_content" | cut -d'|' -f4)
434	
435	            # Validate: same period AND valid number
436	            if [[ "$cached_period_start" == "$period_start" ]] && [[ "$cached_cost" =~ ^[0-9]+\.?[0-9]*$ ]]; then
437	                echo "$cached_cost"
438	                return 0
439	            fi
440	        fi
441	    fi
442	
443	    # Cache miss - calculate from ccusage
444	    # Convert timestamps to ISO format for ccusage
445	    local start_iso=$(timestamp_to_iso "$period_start")
446	    local end_iso=$(timestamp_to_iso "$period_end")
447	
448	    # Get all blocks and filter by monthly period
449	    local blocks_data=$(cd ~ && npx --yes "ccusage@17.1.0" blocks --json --offline 2>/dev/null | awk '/^{/,0')
450	
451	    # Filter blocks within period and sum costs

454	         select(.startTime >= $start and .startTime < $end) |
455	         .costUSD // 0
456	        ] | add // 0

458	
459	    # Format to 2 decimal places
460	    monthly_cost=$(printf "%.2f" "$monthly_cost")
461	
462	    # Atomic cache write with period metadata
463	    local current_ts=$(date +%s)

The statusline is a custom shell script project at `/Users/saurabhtyagi/cc-statusline/`, not the built-in Claude Code statusLine feature. The Claude Code settings at `/Users/saurabhtyagi/.claude/settings.json` simply delegate to that script.

- `sections.show_monthly` is `true` in `/Users/saurabhtyagi/cc-statusline/config/config.json` (line 102).

**The monthly total already resets monthly.** The script does not accumulate indefinitely. Each call to `get_monthly_cost()` filters ccusage blocks whose `startTime` falls within the window `[this month's cycle start, next month's cycle start)`. When the calendar rolls past day 1 of the month (midnight UTC, per that config value), `get_monthly_period()` will compute a new `this_month_cycle` start and the total resets to zero for the new month.

3. There is no separate "monthly reset date" setting to configure — the reset day is implicit in the day component of `payment_cycle_start_date`. If you want it to reset on a different day of the month (e.g., the 15th), you would change `payment_cycle_start_date` to a date whose day component is `15`.
